question: Why does the fresh LOCAL M=8-vs-own-M=1 greedy census read identity 0.4143 / 117 flips when PR #52 banked 0.9966 / 3 (same config, both 512 tok)?

same_config_evidence.note: TPS+PPL reproduce banked to <0.11 TPS / <0.0006 PPL -> same submission/config

methodology_sound.verifier: official greedy_identity, byte-exact token-id, NO tolerance

divergence_is_genuine_near_tie.interpretation: sequences agree ~114 tok then flip at a near-tie; some reconverge, most cascade -> classic batch-shape (M=8 parallel-verify vs M=1 serial) fp non-associativity, NOT a bug

likely_root_cause: M=8 verify processes 8 query positions/step; local vLLM v0.22.1rc1.dev307 captures cudagraph sizes [1,2] (max 2) so the size-8 verify runs EAGER while M=1 (size 1) runs in cudagraph -> divergent numerics. Flip COUNT is environment-sensitive (HF a10g/vLLM build captured 3, local AWS A10G captures 117). PPL is robust to these near-tie flips (still 2.3767).
verdict_impact: NONE on the deploy verdict: deployed is NON-equivalent under BOTH (3 or 117 flips -> outside #407 feasible set, MORE so locally). honest_margin_tps=-14.28 holds (467.14 measured equiv < 481.42 deployed). CAVEAT for the program: local greedy-identity census is NOT an HF-faithful proxy for speculative configs.
